FOREWORD
The 1954 General Assembly imposed upon the Legislative
Research Commission the duty of furnishing the printer correct
copy of bills and resolutions for inclusion in the Kentucky Acts .
Section 57.160 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes stipulates that
bound copies of Kentucky Acts be delivered and distributed
within sixty days after the end of a session .
In the preparation of this volume of the Kentucky Acts of the
Second Extraordinary Session of 1963 the Secretary of State has
furnished access to copy of filed enrolled bills and resolutions .
The staff of the Legislative Research Commission compared the
prepared page proofs with the enrolled bills . Mr. Courtenay J.
Walker , Administrative Assistant to the Director , has had pri-
mary responsibility for the preparation of this volume .
Frankfort , Kentucky
July 3 , 1963
WILSON W. WYATT
Chairman
CHARLES WHEELER
Director

# FOREWORD

Many people helped in the preparation of this volume.

Wilson W. Wyatt, Jr., Assistant to the Director, has had primary responsibility for the preparation of the volume. Mr. Courtenay J. L. has worked with the enrolled bills. The Legislative Research Commission compared the final versions to copies of filed enrolled bills and resolutions.

ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE EXTRAORDINARY SESSION OF THE GENERAL
ASSEMBLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF FRANKFORT ,
KENTUCKY , ON MONDAY , JUNE THE SEVENTEENTH , 1963 , AND
ENDED TUESDAY , JULY THE SECOND , 1963 .
CHAPTER 1
(H. R. 4)
A JOINT RESOLUTION making an appropriation to the Legislative
Research Commission for expenses incurred in connection with the
Second Extraordinary Session of 1963 of the General Assembly , and
declaring an emergency.
WHEREAS , additional personnel are necessary to properly expedite the
business of the Senate and the House of Representatives ; and
WHEREAS , the present appropriation of the Legislative Research Com-
mission is insufficient to provide adequate service to the General
Assembly ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. That there be appropriated out of the General
Expenditure Fund , to the Legislative Sessions Fund , for the pay-
ment of salaries and other expenses of the Second Extraordinary
Session of 1963 of the General Assembly , the sum of nine thousand
dollars per calendar day during the Second Extraordinary Session
of1963 .
Section 2. That there be appropriated out of the General
Expenditure Fund , to the Legislative Research Commission , for
the purpose of providing additional services to the General
Assembly during the Second Extraordinary Session of 1963 of
the General Assembly , the sum of one thousand seven hundred
and fifty dollars per calendar day during the Second Extra-
ordinary Session of 1963 .

2 ACTS , SECOND EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
Section 3. The appropriations made in Sections 1 and 2 of
this resolution shall be available for expenditure during the 1962-
1963 fiscal year and 1963-1964 fiscal year .
Section 4. Whereas , the General Assembly is now in session
and it is necessary that funds be available immediately , an emer-
gency is declared to exist and this resolution shall become effec-
tive upon
itspassage and approval by the Governor
, and shall
beretroactive
toinclude payment
ofcompensation
tosuch per-
sonnel from and including June
17, 1963
.
Approved June 25
, 1963

CHAPTER 2 3
CHAPTER 2
(Н. В. 1)
AN ACT relating to health and welfare , declaring an emergency, and
making an appropriation for medically indigent patient care.
WHEREAS , certain areas of Kentucky will soon be without adequate
hospital facilities due to the announced intention to close certain
existing facilities ; and
WHEREAS , such areas of Kentucky have been repeatedly ravished
by floods and are economically depressed , thereby creating greater
and unusual hazards to the health and welfare of the people ; and
WHEREAS , our greatest resource is our people and their health must
be safeguarded and protected by insuring that adequate hospital
facilities will remain available ;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 205.510 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
As used in KRS 205.510 to 205.610 , in this Act and in 205.991
and 211.106 , unless the context clearly requires a different
meaning :
( 1 ) “Council ” means the Advisory Council for Medical
Assistance ;
(2) "Dentist” means a person authorized to practice den-
tistry under laws of the Commonwealth ;
( 3 ) " Federal -state medical care fund" means a trust and
agency fund established , maintained , and operated by the De-
partment of Economic Security as an arrangement to meet the
cost of medical care for public assistance recipients and recipients
of medical assistance for the aged and into which payments are
made in behalf of each public assistance recipient and recipient of
medical assistance for the aged covered by the fund. Such fund
shall be irrevocable and shall not revert to the General Fund not-
withstanding any provisions of the Budget Act of 1960 to the
contrary ;
(4) "Medical care" as used in this article means essential
medical , surgical , dental , and nursing services , in the home ,
office , clinic , or other suitable place , which are provided or
prescribed by physicians or dentists licensed to render such

4 ACTS , SECOND EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
service , including drugs and medical supplies , appliances , labora-
tory , diagnostic and therapeutic services , nursing home and
convalescent care, hospital care as defined in KRS 205.560
( 1 ) ( a ) , and such other essential medical services and supplies
as may be prescribed by such persons ;
( 5) “Nurses ” means persons authorized to practice pro-
fessional nursing under the laws of the Commonwealth ;
( 6) "Nursing Home " means a facility which provides
routine medical care in which physicians regularly visit patients ,
which provide nursing services and procedures employed in
caring for the sick which require training , judgment , technical
knowledge , and skills beyond that which the untrained person
possesses , and which maintains complete records on patient
care , and which is licensed pursuant to the provisions of KRS
216.400 ;
( 7) "Pharmacist " means a person authorized to practice
pharmacy under the laws of the Commonwealth ;
( 8 ) "Physician " means a person authorized to practice
medicine or osteopathy under the laws of the Commonwealth ;
( 9 ) "Public Assistance Recipient " means a person who has
been certified by the Division of Public Assistance of the Depart-
ment of Economic Security as being eligible for, and a recipient
of, public assistance under the provisions of KRS 205.010 to
205.340 or a needy person who is living with and essential to
such recipient and is recognized in the determination of budg-
etary requirements for such payment ;
( 10 ) "Recipient of Medical Assistance for the Aged " means
an aged person sixty-five years of age and over and one who
resides in this state ( within the meaning of regulations issued
by the Commissioner of Economic Security in conformity with
federal requirements ) , and who is unable through his own income
and resources to provide himself with essential medical care
without depriving himself of necessary food , shelter , clothing ,
or the other necessities of life and who does not fall within the
definition of "Public Assistance Recipient " as defined in sub-
section ( 9 ) of this section and who has been certified by the
Division of Public Assistance of the Department of Economic
Security as meeting the requirements of need ;
(11) "Medically Indigent Patient " means any person
domiciled in this state treated in a nonprofit regional hospital

CHAPTER 2 5
and who is in need of hospital care but who is unable through
his own resources or through resources available to him to pay
the hospitalization costs because of his economic condition and
personal responsibilities and who is not eligible for , nor a
recipient of medical care , under the definition of " Public Assist-
ance Recipient " or " Recipient of Medical Assistance for the
Aged," as set forth in subsections (9) and ( 10) of this section ;
( 12 ) "Nonprofit regional hospital corporation ” means a
nonprofit hospital corporation organized under and pursuant to
the provisions of this Act and in accordance with the method
and manner prescribed by KRS Chapter 273 for the organization
of nonprofit corporations for religious , charitable or educational
purposes;
( 13 ) [ 11 ] "Vendor Payment " means a payment for medical
care which is paid by the Department of Economic Security
directly to the authorized person or institution which rendered
medical care to a public assistance recipient or medical care to a
recipient of medical assistance to the aged [ . ] , or a payment for
hospital care made by the Department to a nonprofit regional
hospital corporation on behalf of a medically indigent patient as
defined in subsection ( 11) of this section .
Section 2. A new section of Chapter 205 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) A nonprofit regional hospital corporation may be or-
ganized under the provisions of this Act and in accordance with
the method and manner prescribed by KRS Chapter 273 for
the organization of nonprofit corporations for religious , charitable
or educational purposes .
( 2 ) The articles of incorporation of every nonprofit
regional hospital corporation shall be presented to the State
Board of Health for approval subject to investigation as provided
in Section 3 of this Act . The approval of the State Board of Health
is a condition precedent for any such articles of incorporation to
be filed in the office of the Secretary of State or recorded in a
County Clerk's office .
Section 3. A new section of Chapter 205 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) The State Board of Health shall prescribe by regulation

6 ACTS , SECOND EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
necessary standards to be met by nonprofit regional hospital
corporations for approval of proposed articles of incorporation
and shall make investigations of the proposed nonprofit regional
hospital corporation to determine whether such standards have
been met and articles of incorporation may be approved . The
State Board of Health shall take into consideration the effect the
proposed nonprofit regional hospital corporation may have upon
hospital service and public health within the territory sought to
be served by it, the public need for the service the corporation
proposes to render , the ability of the corporation to provide and
maintain hospital facilities and to efficiently perform the service
for which approval is requested, the population and economic and
geographic conditions of the area in which the corporation in-
tends to operate and such other factors as the State Board of
Health deems relevant , including but not limited to budgetary
limitations .
Section 4. A new section of Chapter 205 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is created to read as follows :
Every nonprofit regional hospital corporation organized pur-
suant to the provisions of this Act shall be entitled to receive for
each medically indigent patient treated and cared for in the
hospital such hospital care benefits as may be determined by
regulation . The amount of such hospital care benefits and the
manner in which such benefits will be paid shall be determined
by regulations prescribed by the Commissioner of Economic
Security . The Advisory Council on Medical Care shall , from time
to time , make recommendations to the Commissioner of Economic
Security .
Section 5. Section 205.570 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The Department of Economic Security shall retain
all
responsibility relating
tothe certification
ofeligibility
of
allre-
cipients
, including Public Assistance Recipients
, recipients
of
Medical Assistance for the Aged and Medically Indigent Patients
,
fiscal matters
, and general responsibility for the administration
of
KRS 205.510
to205.610
, 205.991 and 211.106
, but shall contract
with the Department
ofHealth for the purpose
ofcarrying
out the medical care aspects
ofthe program
, which aspects shall

CHAPTER 2 7
include but not be limited to the operation functions of certifying
the need of recipients for medical care , issuing authorizations for
provision of medical care , certifying the provision of medical
care in accordance with authorizations issued and in accordance
with quantity and quality standards as established, developing
and maintaining a manual of policies and procedures for the
operation of the medical care aspects of the program , developing
bases of payment for medical care , and certifying vendor billings
for compliance with bases of payment as established pursuant
to KRS 205.510 to 205.610 , 205.991 and 211.106 .
(2) The Commissioner of Economic Security shall have the
authority to issue such regulations as may be necessary and
proper to carry out the provisions of this Act and to comply
with the requirements of Public Law 86-778 , known as the
" Social Security Amendments of 1960 ," and any amendment made
thereto subsequent to 1960 .
Section 6. A new section of Chapter 205 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is created to read as follows :
There is hereby created a fund to be known as the Hospital
Care Fund for the purposes of paying for hospital care on behalf
of medically indigent patients as defined in KRS 205.510 ( 11 ) .
All money appropriated by this State, all money received from
the United States or any agency thereof and
allmoney received
from any other source for the purpose
ofpaying for hospital
care
onbehalf
ofmedically indigent patients
,
asdefined herein
,
are hereby appropriated and shall
beavailable only for vendor
payments for hospital care for medically indigent patients
.
Section
7.
Anew section
ofChapter 205
ofKentucky Re-
vised Statutes
iscreated to read as follows
:
Fiscal Courts and city legislative bodies are hereby author-
ized
tocontract with any nonprofit regional hospital corporation
organized pursuant

of
the residents

patients who are

tothis section by fiscal courts and incorporated cities

8 ACTS , SECOND EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
Section 8. ( 1) To carry out the purposes of this Act ,
there is hereby appropriated out of the General Expenditure
Fund in the State Treasury the sum of Seven Hundred Thousand
Dollars ($700,000 ) for the 1963-64 fiscal year .
(2) Notwithstanding the provisions of the 1962 appropri-
ations Act
allgeneral fund appropriations made under Part One
,

CHAPTER 3 9
CHAPTER 3
(S. R. 4 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION ratifying a proposed amendment to the Consti-
tution of the United States .
WHEREAS , Senate Joint Resolution 29 of the Eighty- Seventh Congress
(Second Session ) proposes an amendment to the Constitution of
the United States ; and
WHEREAS , the amendment so proposed shall be valid to all intents
and purposes as part of the Constitution of the United States if
ratified by the legislatures of three -fourths of the several states
within seven years from the date of its submission by the Congress ;
and
WHEREAS , the purpose of the proposed amendment is in keeping with
the desires of the people of this Commonwealth to promote and
protect the free exercise of the ballot by all citizens of the United
States;
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. The amendment to the Constitution of the
United States proposed by Senate Joint Resolution 29 of the
Eighty -Seventh Congress (Second Session ), reading substantially
as follows , hereby is ratified :
"Article-
"Section 1. The right of citizens of the United States
to vote in any primary or other election for President or
Vice President , for electors for President or Vice President ,
or for Senator or Representative in Congress , shall not be
denied or abridged by the United States or any State by
reason of failure to pay any poll tax or other tax.
"Sec . 2. The Congress shall have power to enforce
this article by appropriate legislation ."
Section 2. The Governor of the Commonwealth shall cause
certified copies of this resolution to be sent to the Administrator
of General Services of the United States , to the presiding officer
of the United States Senate, and to the Speaker of the House of
Representatives of the United States .
Approved July 2, 1963

10 ACTS , SECOND EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
CHAPTER 4
(S. B. 1)
AN ACT relating to penal corrections .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Commonwealth
of Kentucky :
Section 1. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
The Commission on Correction and Community Service shall
consist of the Commissioner of the Department of Corrections ,
the Director of the Division of Probation and Parole , the Director
of the Division of Institutions , the Chairman of the Parole Board ,
two circuit judges designated by the Governor , and five public
members appointed by the Governor , these to include a psy-
chiatrist , a professional educator , a member who is qualified
to express the views of business and industry , a member who is
qualified to express the views of organized labor, and a practic-
ing attorney . The judicial and public members shall serve for
the remainder of the term of office of the Governor during whose
incumbency they were appointed , unless sooner removed for
cause ;
allother members shall serve during their terms
ofoffice
.
The Commissioner of Corrections shall act
aschairman of the
Commission
. All members of the Commission shall serve with-
out compensation
, but each member shall
bereimbursed for his
necessary travel and other expenses actually incurred
inthe
discharge
ofhis duties on the Commission
. For administrative
and budgetary purposes
, the Commission
isattached
tothe De-
partment
ofCorrections
, which shall furnish necessary staff
services
tothe Commission
.
Section
2.
Anew section
ofthe Kentucky Revised Statutes
iscreated to read as follows
:
(
1) The Commission shall meet
atleast every three months
,
and whenever called into session by the chairman
,
atthe request
ofthe Governor
,
ofany two
ormore members
ofthe Commission

or on his own motion

(2) The Commission shall advise the Governor and the
Commissioner
ofCorrections concerning correctional policy and
programs
, including particularly the following
:

CHAPTER 4 11
(a) the need for , and the development of new or specialized
institutions , facilities , or programs ;
(b) the need for , and the effectuation of collaboration and
liaison within the Department , and between the Department and
community agencies and resources , including the bench and bar ,
in order to promote the readjustment and rehabilitation of
offenders in institutions or under parole or probation supervision
in the community ;
(c) the need for, and the development of useful researches
in penology , correctional treatment , criminal law, or in the dis-
ciplines relevant thereto .
Section 3. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
The Commission or one or more of
itsmembers may visit
and inspect any state penal institution

relating

12 ACTS , SECOND EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
( 2 ) The members of the board shall give full time to the
duties of their office and shall receive necessary traveling ex-
penses and not less than [ $7,500 ] $ 10,000 per annum compen-
sation, except the chairman of the board shall receive additional
compensation of $500 per year for his services . Their terms of
office shall be four years and until their successors are appointed
and have qualified ( , ) . (except that the first three members
shall be appointed for terms of two , three and four years , respec-
tively . ] Their successors shall be appointed thereafter as pro-
vided in this section for terms of four years, and a vacancy
occurring before expiration of the term of office shall be similarly
filled for the unexpired term . The chairman of the board shall
serve as such until the expiration of his term at which time the
Governor shall name his successor and designate the chairman
of the board . If a vacancy occurs in the chairmanship of the
board before the expiration of the term the Governor may name
a successor to serve for the remainder of the unexpired term .
( 3 ) [Two ] Three members of the board shall constitute a
quorum [ , ] . [but if two members be absent during any work-
ing day then the director may serve in their stead and his
presence in such service may be counted for determining a
quorum. ] The organization of the board shall be determined
by the chairman .
(4) The Governor may not remove any member of the
board except for disability , inefficiency , neglect of duty, or mal-
feasance in office . Before such removal he shall give the member
a written copy of the charges against him and shall fix the time
when he can be heard in his defense , which shall not be less
than ten days thereafter . Upon removal the Governor shall file
in the office of the Secretary of State a complete statement of
all
charges made against the member and the findings thereupon
with

14 ACTS , SECOND EXTRAORDINARY SESSION
Section 7. For the purpose of administering this Act and
to upgrade the quality and quantity of technical and professional
personnel services there is hereby appropriated to the Depart-
ment of Corrections out of the general expenditure fund in the
State Treasury the sum of one hundred twenty -five thousand dol-
lars ( $ 125,000 ) for the 1963-64 fiscal year .
Section 8. A new section of Chapter 197 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is created to read as follows :
( 1 ) A sentence , on conviction of a felony , imposed upon
a confined prisoner for a crime committed prior to the date of
his instant commitment , if designated to be served consecutively ,
shall be added to the sentence or sentences being served.
( 2 ) If the additional sentence is designated to be served
concurrently , or the commitment is silent , he shall be considered
as having started to serve said sentence on the day he was com-
mitted on the first sentence .
Section 9. Sections 439.115 , 439.125 , 439.135 , 439.145 ,
439.155 and 439.165 and subsection ( 4) of KRS 196.070 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes are repealed .
Section 10. This Act becomes effective October 1 , 1963 .
Approved July 2, 1963
